gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 found one P2 compatibility issue: the initial legacy wrapper omitted SourceDocument, which had previously been available from restaurant_kv_serving.benchmarks due to a module-scope import. The wrapper now re-exports SourceDocument for legacy direct and star imports, while document_kv_cache.benchmarks keeps its curated benchmark __all__. Final GPT-5.5 review found no remaining findings.
scope: document and legacy package README migration notes
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_benchmarks.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_benchmark_runner.py tests/test_dataset_prep.py tests/test_release_evidence.py -q
python -m py_compile src/document_kv_cache/benchmarks.py src/restaurant_kv_serving/benchmarks.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py
find src tests -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 python -m py_compile
what_changed: Moved the V1 benchmark contract implementation into document_kv_cache.benchmarks.
Turned restaurant_kv_serving.benchmarks into a compatibility wrapper over the document benchmark module.
Preserved the legacy SourceDocument module-scope export while keeping document_kv_cache.benchmarks.__all__ curated.
Updated package READMEs and public-package tests to record the new implementation boundary.
why: The generalized package should own its V1 benchmark contract directly instead of routing new document imports through the legacy restaurant package. This is a small behavior-preserving migration step toward retiring restaurant-specific implementation ownership.
